hostControl.intro: Manage the printer's Linux host — check its health and OS state and control its background services. Disk cleanup and system settings arrive in later phases.

help.title: What is this?
help.body: FilaMind runs on the printer's small Linux computer (the host). This panel reads that machine's live health and OS state so you can spot problems — an overheating CPU, a full disk, an unsynced clock — without opening a terminal, and lets you control the background services it runs.
help.step1: Read the cards for CPU temperature, memory, disk and network at a glance.
help.step2: Watch the top-processes table to see what is using the CPU right now.
help.step3: Leave auto-refresh on for a live view, or turn it off and refresh manually.

monitor.title: Reading the monitor
monitor.body: Everything here is read-only. Bars turn amber past 75% and red past 90%. CPU temperature turns amber at 70 °C and red at 80 °C. The throttle warning only appears on boards that report it (mainly Raspberry Pi).

services.title: Managing services
services.body: Each row is a systemd service. Click one to start/stop/restart it, enable/disable it at boot, mask it, read its log, or — for services you installed yourself — delete its unit file. Stopping or disabling a service takes a typed or one-tap confirmation. Critical and protected services are marked; protected ones (like SSH and this panel) can't be stopped, to avoid locking you out.

host.def: The small Linux computer (e.g. a Raspberry Pi or BTT CB1) that runs Klipper, Moonraker and FilaMind — separate from the printer's microcontrollers.

load.def: How many processes were waiting to run, averaged over 1 / 5 / 15 minutes. Roughly: a value near the core count means the CPU is busy; well above it means it is overloaded.

swap.def: Disk space used as overflow when RAM fills up. Heavy swap use slows the host down — a sign it is short on memory.

throttle.def: When the board reduces speed to protect itself — usually from an under-powered supply (under-voltage) or overheating. Reported mainly on Raspberry Pi.

ntp.def: Network Time Protocol keeps the host clock accurate. An unsynced clock can break timelapses, logs and TLS certificates.

locale.def: The host's language and keyboard layout settings, used by the OS for text, dates and the console keymap.

service.def: A background program managed by systemd — Klipper, Moonraker, the webcam streamer and the network stack all run as services.

enabled.def: Whether a service starts automatically at boot. 'enabled' starts on boot; 'disabled' doesn't; 'static' is only started by another service.

mask.def: A stronger form of disable — the service can't be started at all (even by another service) until it's unmasked. Use with care.

services.warning: Stopping or disabling the wrong service can take the printer offline. Critical services are marked; protected ones can't be stopped.
services.deletePrompt: This permanently removes the unit file. Type "{name}" to confirm.
